name: AI Agents Test and DrupalForge testing template
summary: Drupal-native agent tool-behavior test suites plus a hosted Drupal CMS template that runs them on a site.

provenance.note: AI Agents Test and its DrupalForge template are grouped because the hosted page points to the module; the suite pointer is pinned to the module's shipped Drupal CMS YAML examples.

artifacts.name: Drupal CMS agent tool-behavior YAML suites
artifacts.question_answered: Does a Drupal-native agent select the expected tools, avoid forbidden tools, respect tool order, and pass parameter checks for the supplied prompts?
artifacts.mapping_note: Most rules deterministically inspect tool presence, absence, order, and parameters; optional rules can ask an LLM to score a tool parameter or text response. Drupal-native agents must remain a separate class from external coding agents.
artifacts.claim_boundary: The shipped rules do not independently inspect final Drupal state, so a passing suite does not prove that the requested content or configuration outcome actually exists.

artifacts.name: Hosted Drupal CMS AI Agents Testing Framework
artifacts.question_answered: Can the Drupal-native agent tool-behavior suites be exercised on a real hosted Drupal CMS runtime with inspectable run results?
artifacts.mapping_note: The hosted page is substrate prior art, not a task mapping; it states that tests mutate actual content and are not easily rerun without reset or cleanup.
artifacts.claim_boundary: A convenient hosted template is not a pinned, reset-proven evaluation substrate and does not make its displayed results comparable to local Agent Readiness runs.
